items.title: Dozens of Aboriginal language custodians call for further revival efforts

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

gallery.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

thumbnail.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

items.title: Alleged Mongols associate arrested in rural Darwin after days-long manhunt

children.content: Northern Territory police officers have arrested Isaac Thompson, 26, in Darwin River, more than 48 hours after he escaped custody during a medical transfer.

image.altText: White man with dark brown hair, brown eyes, thick brown eyebrows with a moustache and beard.

items.title: NT minister claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns

children.content: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

image.altText: A female politician sitting down and smiling, inside a parliamentary chamber.

items.title: Single mother of six hit with $6k power bill after months of no communication

children.content: A single mother of six young children is paying off a $6,000 power bill after the Northern Territory's main energy retailer failed to send her a bill for almost 12 months. She's among thousands of Jacana Energy customers facing significant bills.

image.altText: A woman with straight brown shoulder length hair in a black tshirt, sitting at her kitchen table reading her bills.

items.title: Native title holders celebrate return of 'special country' in access win

children.content: Traditional owners from the Utopia region north of Alice Springs celebrate the recognition of their native title over an area encompassing more than 4,000 square kilometres.

image.altText: A federal court judge hands native title documents to native title holders.

items.title: A bus, a solar-powered stage and a journey across remote Australia

children.content: The Guts tour is a roaming, solar-powered convoy that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush.

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

image.altText: podcast image NAIDOC week

articles.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

gallery.altText: Four people stand in a spring surrounded by tropical trees and wetland vegetation.
Person plays a drum kit as children gather around during a live music performance.
Tour bus drives along a remote dirt road beside a large termite mound.

items.title: Alice Springs tourism operators celebrate funding after toughest days

children.content: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

image.altText: A man and a woman standing together in warm clothing

items.title: Man, 34, in custody after girl, 17, found dead in remote NT home

children.content: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Galiwin'ku on Elcho Island.

items.title: Remote NT MLA admits to claiming travel allowance during stays at city home

children.content: Manuel Brown's dumping from the role comes after weeks of scrutiny over his driving record and confirmation he allowed two of his underage children to drive his personal car on an outstation.

gallery.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

thumbnail.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

items.title: ABC rejects claims of antisemitic bias ahead of fronting royal commission

children.content: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

image.altText: A sign shown on the outside of a large building.

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals are upon us. Here's what you need to know

children.content: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.

image.altText: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham celebrate an England goal

items.title: More than 120 NSW crown prosecutors take rare step over DPP report

children.content: Senior crown prosecutor Brett Hatfield SC issued the extraordinary statement on behalf of the more than 120 barristers who take on cases for the crown.

image.altText: a woman in legal dress leaving a court

items.title: Australian 'alien' arrested after ICE investigation, Trump says

children.content: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

image.altText: A woman with brown hair smiles while wearing a dotty black shirt.

items.title: This Place: Djimubarn, sacred rock art shelter

children.content: In Western Arnhem Land the Markani estate of the Madjawarr clan is full of stories.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: A woman wearing a cap speaks about the art inside a rocky overhang

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a cap speaks about the art inside a rocky overhang

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing a cap speaks about the art inside a rocky overhang

items.title: RBA chief economist warns of more frequent supply shocks

children.content: The Reserve Bank's Sarah Hunter says global supply shocks appear to be occurring more frequently and the RBA will try to adjust to the new reality.

image.altText: RBA HQ with entrance boarded up due to renovations, viewed through trees

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

children.content: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

image.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

children.content: The WA Police commissioner writes to Meta to ask for assistance with Facebook moderation after a number of derogatory comments are left on social media pages.

image.altText: A medium close-up shot of WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch in uniform.

items.title: Court told former MP knew lying was 'part and parcel' of visa scheme

children.content: A court hears former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire knew being dishonest to the Department of Immigration was a component of an alleged fraudulent visa scheme.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit, white shirt and tie looks towards the camera in an urban environment.

items.title: Millions of tiny engineers help to restore health of marine ecosystem in WA

children.content: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

image.altText: underwater shot of diver checking ropes full of mussels

items.title: Quiz: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world

children.content: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

image.altText: Aerial of Capetown

items.title: Aussie doubles fly the flag at Wimbledon as mixed pair makes final

children.content: Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter win through to the Wimbledon mixed doubles final, as Thanasi Kokkinakis flies the flag in the men's doubles.

articles.title: Djokovic wins five-hour Wimbledon epic to set up Sinner blockbuster

children.content: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic sets up a Wimbledon semifinal blockbuster against Jannik Sinner after a five-hour epic.

image.altText: GettyImages-2284558415

articles.title: 'I'm getting old': 28yo Osaka's Wimbledon run ends at quarterfinals

children.content: Japan's Naomi Osaka bemoans getting old as her inspiring Wimbledon run comes to an end in the quarterfinals.

image.altText: A tennis player grimaces at the sky while standing in front of a clock.

gallery.altText: A tennis player plays a shot close to the net.

thumbnail.altText: A tennis player plays a shot close to the net.

items.title: Bondi survivor says YouTube's 'false flag' video decision 'inexplicable'

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky has criticised a decision by YouTube to keep a video on its platform, in which creators suggested he had lied about his injuries from the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky outside a building wearing a suit and blue patterned tie

items.title: Woman at centre of university corruption claims fronts ICAC inquiry

children.content: Former University of Wollongong chief governance officer Alyssa White takes the witness box at the ICAC inquiry after weeks of evidence examining her role in recruitment decisions, governance reforms and a proposed executive position.

image.altText: Woman walking alongside lawyer

items.title: Foreign investor snub of divestment order 'extraordinary', says expert

children.content: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

image.altText: A remote mining camp in the outback

items.title: Proposed building code throws Qld construction industry into 'confusion'

children.content: Queensland Council of Unions general secretary Jacqueline King has called on Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie to explain the proposed changes to the industry.

image.altText: Two cranes against a blue sky.

items.title: 'I want to clear my name': Former NSW minister charged with misconduct

children.content: Former New South Wales Liberal government minister John Sidoti says he will fight a charge of misconduct while in public office.

thumbnail.altText: a man standing in a doorway looking

items.title: Video shows men who attended Khamenei's funeral and the man who didn't

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men it sees as central to it.

image.altText: Iran's leadership team appeared at Ali Khamenei's funeral.

items.title: Qld business fined $1,200 after sparking million-dollar fire ant scare

children.content: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

image.altText: mound of dirt with ants on it

items.title: With women's game short on resources this AFLW player went to Doha for ACL rehab

children.content: Essendon AFLW forward Emily Gough said devastating ACL injuries can get lost in the sheer amount of them in women's game.

image.altText: Essendon AFLW player went to Doha for specialised ACL rehabilitation.

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer will sit next match out after death

children.content: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

image.altText: Epping Football Netball Club president Luke Di Vincentis stands near the boundary fence

items.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says

children.content: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

thumbnail.altText: A factory and furnace against the backdrop of sunset and a lake in the distance

items.title: Alleged fish poachers fined during comp in 'blatant' cheating attempt

children.content: The group was caught allegedly fishing illegally in a no-take zone during a competition. Four people have been fined almost $15,000.

image.altText: A boat out on blue water in front of an island

items.title: Netanyahu warns Trump against F-35 deal with Türkiye

children.content: The Israeli prime minister has appeared on American TV to urge the US president not to sell jet fighters to Türkiye, adding to a growing list of tensions between the leaders.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stand shoulder to shoulder and pose for a photo at the entrance of the White House.

items.title: The 'amazing' turnaround in literacy at this primary school

children.content: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.
